description:
Advances: specled.triangulation

## Repro

After populating a subject's `realized_by.implementation:` with any MFA (e.g. `SpecLedEx.Status.build/3`), run:

    mix spec.triangle specled.status

Observed:

    ** (FunctionClauseError) no function clause matching in Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.mfa_source_file/1

        The following arguments were given to Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.mfa_source_file/1:

            # 1
            "SpecLedEx.Coverage.category_summary/3"

        Attempted function clauses (showing 1 out of 1):

            defp mfa_source_file({mod, _fun, _arity})

        (spec_led_ex 0.1.0) lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex:288: Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.mfa_source_file/1
        (elixir 1.18.1) lib/enum.ex:4438: Enum.flat_map_list/2
        (spec_led_ex 0.1.0) lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex:247: Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.build_requirement_view/4
        (spec_led_ex 0.1.0) lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex:207: Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.build_diagnostic/5
        (spec_led_ex 0.1.0) lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex:68: Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.run/1

## Root cause

`lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex`, `build_requirement_view/4` (lines 237-248):

```elixir
closure_mfas =
  if closure do
    (closure.owned_mfas ++ closure.shared_mfas)
    |> Enum.map(&mfa_to_string/1)          # tuples -> strings
  else
    []
  end

closure_files =
  closure_mfas
  |> Enum.flat_map(&mfa_source_file/1)      # expects tuples, gets strings
  |> Enum.uniq()
```

`closure_mfas` is mapped to strings via `mfa_to_string/1` on line 240. Then on line 247 those strings are passed to `mfa_source_file/1`, which is defined as `defp mfa_source_file({mod, _fun, _arity})` (line 288) — a tuple-only head. No string clause, no fallback, so the first string blows up with FunctionClauseError.

## Why it did not surface in the q59.6 test suite

Before `realized_by.implementation:` entries were populated, `Closure.compute/2` had no seed bindings for any subject, so `closure` was `nil` everywhere. The `if closure do` branch on line 238 short-circuited and `closure_mfas` was always `[]`, so `mfa_source_file/1` was never called. The q59.6 tests exercised `findings/3` directly with pre-built `closure_map` values; they did not run the Mix task end-to-end against a real spec workspace with populated implementation bindings.

The branch `feature/realized-by-triangle-closure` landed 31 `realized_by:` blocks on subjects. Several carry `implementation:` MFAs that produce non-nil closures, which is when this path first fires in practice.

## Fix sketch

Two reasonable fixes:

**Option A (cleaner)** — keep `closure_mfas` as tuples internally, stringify only for display:

```elixir
closure_mfas_tuples =
  if closure, do: closure.owned_mfas ++ closure.shared_mfas, else: []

closure_files =
  closure_mfas_tuples
  |> Enum.flat_map(&mfa_source_file/1)
  |> Enum.uniq()

closure_mfas_display = Enum.map(closure_mfas_tuples, &mfa_to_string/1)

%{
  id: req_id,
  # ...
  closure_mfas: closure_mfas_display,
  closure_files: closure_files
}
```

**Option B** — add a string clause to `mfa_source_file/1` that parses via `SpecLedEx.Realization.Binding.parse/1`:

```elixir
defp mfa_source_file(mfa) when is_binary(mfa) do
  case SpecLedEx.Realization.Binding.parse(mfa) do
    {:ok, {mod, fun, arity}} -> mfa_source_file({mod, fun, arity})
    {:ok, {:module, mod}} -> mfa_source_file({mod, nil, nil})
    _ -> []
  end
end
```

Option A is preferred — the real issue is that the map/stringify step on line 240 was premature. The task has a display concern and a lookup concern and the original code conflated them.

## Tests to add

- Unit test for `mix spec.triangle <subject>` where the subject has a populated `realized_by.implementation:` binding that produces a non-nil closure. Assert it does not raise and prints per-requirement diagnostic including `closure_files:` with resolved source paths.
- Regression test at the `build_requirement_view/4` seam: call with a stub closure containing `owned_mfas: [{Enum, :map, 2}]`, assert the view's `closure_files` contains the Enum source path.
- Add the test as part of `test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs` (already present, 3 tests from q59.6).

## Impact

Any `mix spec.triangle <subject>` invocation crashes for subjects that declare `realized_by.implementation:` bindings AND have at least one owned/shared MFA in the closure walk. With the 31 `realized_by` blocks now on main's feature branch, this is most subjects. Users who try the read-only diagnostic hit an immediate FunctionClauseError with no actionable output.

## Notes

- This is NOT related to ticket specled_-q59.9 (wire tiers into `mix spec.check`). `spec.triangle` is a standalone read-only diagnostic that already runs independently. Fixing this does not require the orchestrator wiring.
- Also unrelated to specled_-kox (the suggester's miscamelization bug) — different task, different code path.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: b420def2-f093-480d-8e4a-4b0986194549
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b420def2-f093-480d-8e4a-4b0986194549.jsonl

Commit: 9772f97 (branch specled_-btd/fix-mfa-source-file-string, worktree /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-btd)
Files touched:
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex
- test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs
Tests added:
- Mix.Tasks.SpecTriangleTest: "does not crash when closure is non-nil and prints resolved closure files"
Verification stubs flipped: none (spec-verification stubs for specled.triangulation were already execute: true)

Summary: Applied fix Option A from the ticket. Kept closure MFAs as tuples for the mfa_source_file/1 lookup and moved the mfa_to_string/1 step onto a separate lane so it only drives display. Added --xref-path option (symmetric with --artifact-path) so the regression test can feed a fabricated tracer edges manifest into the non-nil closure code path without touching shared _build state. Without the fix the new test reproduces the exact FunctionClauseError from the ticket; with the fix all 4 triangle task tests and the full 529-test suite pass, and mix spec.check reports status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 findings=0.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: unavailable-in-subagent
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/unavailable-in-subagent.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (bug-fix commit): 2
- Files outside expected scope (lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs): 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 4, passed: 4 (15 + 2 + 3 + 4 = 24 tests total, 0 failures)
- specled.triangulation.spec_triangle_task has a passing test that exercises the non-nil closure path: yes ("does not crash when closure is non-nil and prints resolved closure files" — feeds a fabricated xref ETF so Closure.compute/2 returns non-nil, driving mfa_source_file/1 on tuples)
- Temporarily reverting the fix makes the new test fail with FunctionClauseError: yes (reproduced the exact crash: `no function clause matching in Mix.Tasks.Spec.Triangle.mfa_source_file/1` with argument `"SpecLedEx.index/3"` at spec.triangle.ex:300 via build_requirement_view/4)
- Project verification (`mix spec.check`): pass (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 findings=0)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Fix applies Option A from the ticket (preferred): closure MFAs stay as tuples for mfa_source_file/1 lookup; mfa_to_string/1 is now applied on a separate display lane. This addresses the root cause rather than patching around it (no string clause added to mfa_source_file/1).
- The commit also adds a `--xref-path` CLI option (symmetric with `--artifact-path`), wired through load_tracer_edges/1. This is mild scope expansion beyond the strict "Fix sketch" — it exists to let the regression test drive a non-nil closure via a fabricated tracer manifest in an isolated tmp root without touching shared _build state. Justified and documented in the moduledoc.
- load_tracer_edges/0 became load_tracer_edges/1; no external callers so the arity change is self-contained.
- After temporary revert for step 9, the file was restored cleanly (git diff empty).

title: fix: mix spec.triangle passes MFA strings to mfa_source_file/1 which expects a tuple
